name: Portugal: Piri-Piri Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the olive oil into a small, screw-top jar.
2. Add the chopped chilli peppers, bay leaf, and lemon rind to the jar.
3. Secure the lid tightly on the jar.
4. Shake well to combine all ingredients.
5. Allow the mixture to stand in a warm place for at least 24 hours before using.
6. Store the prepared piri-piri sauce at room temperature indefinitely.
Ready-made piri-piri sauce can be purchased by Portuguese individuals and is commonly used as a spicy condiment on pasta, fish, or meat dishes, as well as for flavoring stews.
